scenario_title: Add then delete the identical RR within one UPDATE
test_rationale: This test targets the underspecified interaction between RFC 2136 add and delete semantics when both operations name the exact same RR in a single UPDATE message. Because the protocol text defines each operation separately but not their composition, different servers may either collapse the pair, apply it literally, or reject it. A follow-up QUERY on the touched RRset makes the final state observable, while the UPDATE reply itself can also distinguish implementations.

group_1.behavior: Processes the UPDATE sequentially and returns success, leaving the RR absent after the delete_rr.

group_2.behavior: Treats the add-then-delete-of-identical-RR sequence as a conflicting or invalid UPDATE and rejects it.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4.2 and §3.4.3 define add_rr and delete_rr individually, but do not specify how a server must handle an exact add/delete pair for the same RR inside one UPDATE. That leaves room for canonicalization to no-op, sequential application, or rejection, producing divergent reply codes and/or final RRset state.
